assets.name: CAD Dividend Stocks
why: Globe and Mail (June 30, 2026): 23% earnings growth expected over next 12 months for S&P/TSX; elevated valuations moderate price gains despite strong earnings
RBC/Digrin (July 2026): Dividend growth averaging 7.57% annually with 2.36% yield; mature equity income returning to normalized 5-7% dividend growth trajectory
Motley Fool Canada (April 2026): utilities like Fortis expecting 5% annual cash flow growth and infrastructure companies targeting mid-single-digit returns in normalized rate environment

assets.name: US Dividend Stocks
why: Goldman Sachs (May 2026): 6% S&P 500 price target + 6% dividend growth + ~2% yield = ~9-10% total return in 2026
Morgan Stanley (May 2026): 12% earnings growth + 2% dividend yield projecting ~8-9% total return 2027
Goldman Sachs 10-year modal forecast (Nov 2025): 7.7% annualized returns with moderation as valuations stabilize by 2028

assets.name: Gold
why: Goldman Sachs $4,900, JPMorgan $4,500 Q4 2026 targets; base midpoint $4,700 implies +18.1% from $3,980 spot (July 2026)
Goldman Sachs $5,400-5,600 & JPMorgan $5,400 EOY 2027 targets; consensus $5,350 implies +13.8% from $4,700 base
Extended forecasts 2028 range $5,500-6,500; structural central bank buying supports $5,800 estimate, implying +8.4% from $5,350 base

assets.name: Bitcoin (BTC)
why: 20.0%: Standard Chartered (Dec 2025) projects $150K by end-2026 vs. ~$65K current; mid-range Bernstein/Bitwise targets $150-200K
12.5%: Bernstein $200K (2027 cycle peak) and Standard Chartered $225K imply moderate acceleration with maturing ETF demand
10.0%: Standard Chartered $300K (2028) implies deceleration; JPMorgan long-term thesis ($266K+) suggests 2028 still early in institutional adoption curve

assets.name: Ethereum (ETH)
why: Standard Chartered Jan 2026: $15,000 target 2027; current $1.6K base yields ~100% for institutional adoption recovery
Standard Chartered Jan 2026: $22,000 target 2028; layered growth from stablecoin/RWA expansion; ~80% normalized
Standard Chartered Jan 2026: $30,000+ 2029 target; structural tokenization tailwinds; 45% accounts for consolidation risk

assets.name: Canadian REITs
why: RBC 2026 base case: high-single-digit returns (8-9%) from 4-6% FFOPU growth + 7% NAV gains + 5% dividend (Pammi Bir, Dec 2025)
RBC 2027 outlook: 11% earnings CAGR through 2027 for seniors housing and steady industrial growth support 7% total return as rate environment stabilizes (RBC, June 2026)
2028 moderation to 6% as supply-demand dynamics normalize and sector reaches mature growth; reflects reasonable leverage and low-to-high-single-digit NAV growth trajectory (RBC, April 2026)

assets.name: Canadian Real Estate
why: CREA July 15, 2026 forecast: 1.1% price growth in 2026 (published 2 days ago)
CREA July 15, 2026 forecast: 1.1% price growth in 2027 (published 2 days ago)
Derived from CREA's stated policy that gains remain below 2% inflation target; BoC expects ~2% inflation in 2028, supporting subdued ~0.9% home price growth

assets.name: US Real Estate
why: CBRE Capital Markets outlook Feb 2026: 5-15 bps cap compression + income-driven returns; multifamily averaging 5.6% cap rate Q1 2026 (Rentana June 2026)
Cushman & Wakefield Jan 2026: Rent growth forecast to strengthen to 5% by 2027; modest cap compression continues with improving fundamentals
Cohen & Steers Dec 2025: 2026 shows muted asset value growth with income driving returns; normalized 5.5% yield + moderate property appreciation

assets.name: Canadian Bonds
why: RBC (July 2026): 10Y GoC yield ~3.6% by end 2026; modest coupons offset by rising yield headwinds
Scotiabank (July 2026): GoC 5Y yield forecast to 3.25% by Q4 2026, rate hikes to 3.0% by Q1 2027; coupons ~3.2% with stabilizing prices
BoC (July 2026): GDP growth 1.8% in 2028, core inflation near target; yields likely stable, full coupon benefit realized

assets.name: Canadian HY Bonds
why: Year 1: 5.2% - BlackRock's 5.7% HY forecast moderated for tight Canadian spreads noted by RBC (Dec 2025) and current elevated yield environment providing near-term income support.

Year 2: 4.8% - Mid-range of J.P. Morgan's 6.1% longer-term HY view and Vanguard's 4.3-5.3% range, reflecting stabilization after near-term carry exhaustion and potential spread widening as RBC warned (June 2026).

Year 3: 4.5% - Conservative anchor reflecting RBC's cautious stance on Canadian credit (June 2026) and normalized carry yield as elevated rate environment moderates; aligns with long-term fixed-income yield-return correlation.

assets.name: US Tech Stocks
why: Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Deutsche Bank consensus: 17% S&P 500 return in 2026 (May-July 2026 reports)
Morgan Stanley mid-2027 target of 8,300 from ~7,550 implies ~10% appreciation; earnings growth decelerates to 12% in 2027 (Morgan Stanley, May 2026)
Morgan Stanley projects 13% earnings growth for 2028 with stable 20-21x valuation multiples; deceleration reflects normalized growth post-AI capex cycle (Morgan Stanley, May 2026)

assets.name: HISA (Canada)
why: BoC holds 2.25% through 2026, mainstream HISA non-promotional rates averaging 1.5-2.2% per NerdWallet/RateHub July 15, 2026
Consensus BoC rate rises to ~2.75% by end-2027 (RBC 3.25%, Scotiabank 3%, CIBC 2.75%, BMO/TD 2.25%), pushing HISA spreads to 2.25-2.75%; using mid-consensus, National Bank July 2026 forecast
BoC policy rate stabilizes 2.75-3.0% in 2028; HISA rates flatten at 2.25-2.50% range as deposit competition normalizes, per True North Mortgage July 2026 outlook

assets.name: Intl Dividend Stocks
why: Goldman Sachs forecasts global equities 11% for 2026, with international diversification benefiting from weaker USD and earnings growth; EAFE dividend stocks likely trail global slightly at ~8.5% (GS, Jan 2026)
Goldman Sachs 10-year Europe forecast 7.1% with dividends/shareholder returns as half the return; moderate pullback in year 2 as valuations stabilize suggests ~7.5% (GS, Nov 2025)
Long-term Goldman Sachs Europe baseline 7.1% with 'half earnings, half shareholder return' reflects normalized trend; EAFE dividend-focused returns converge to this as growth moderates (GS, Nov 2025)

assets.name: Emerging Markets
why: Goldman Sachs Research explicitly forecasts 16% EM returns for 2026, December 2025
Moderating earnings growth and fading currency tailwinds post-2026; normalize toward 10% supported by still-above-trend EM GDP growth (4%+)
Further normalization toward historical average; maturation of AI cycle tailwinds and normalization of macro conditions limit upside

assets.name: Silver
why: J.P. Morgan average forecast of $81/oz for 2026 (vs. $57.48 current), implying 40.8% return; forecast published Feb 2026
J.P. Morgan $85/oz 2027 average with year-end implication of $95/oz (4.9% from $91 2027 entry), split with structural deficit thesis from Silver Institute via GoldSilver April 2026
LongForecast projects $98-103 opening 2028 reaching $132 year-end (18-20% range), supported by CoinPriceForecast $176-212 range suggesting $100+ conservative entry; published May-June 2026

assets.name: Palladium
why: 2026: LBMA consensus $1,740/oz avg (51% vs 2025), BofA $1,725, TD $1,819; near-term support from Russian supply risks & hybrid vehicle demand per TD Securities Jan 2026
2027: WalletInvestor $2,368 y/e (47.4% gain), WPIC deficits persist through 2027, hybrid/autocat demand resilient; TD Securities noted PGMs lead in 2026-27
2028: WPIC projects surplus emergence from 2028f, EV adoption 22-25% (J2T Jan 26), recycling growth to 3.5-3.8M oz offsets mine supply; Long Forecast $1,668 implies price pressure

assets.name: WTI Crude Oil
why: EIA July 2026 STEO forecasts 2027 average ~$64/bbl; Goldman Sachs June 2026 reset points to $75 Brent equilibrium by 2027, implying ~22% decline from current ~$90 spot as Hormuz disruption normalizes
Goldman Sachs June 2026 outlook shows $75–$80/bbl 2027 average with structural oversupply moderating; modest rebound from 2027 lows drives ~7% recovery in Year 2
Goldman Sachs Projects Brent/WTI gradually recovering to $80/$76 by late 2028 per their 2026 Outlooks; structural demand growth and supply constraints support ~4% annualized gain in Year 3

assets.name: Natural Gas
why: EIA July 2026 STEO projects Henry Hub $3.67/MMBtu in 2026, +28.8% from $2.85 mid-year baseline; tight balances from new LNG exports
EIA July 2026 STEO projects $3.49/MMBtu in 2027, -5.1% from 2026 as record production and above-average inventory offset demand growth
Consensus 2028: S&P Global $3.50, BMI/Fitch $4.00; midpoint $3.75 = +7.4% as LNG terminals reach full capacity and demand-supply tightens

assets.name: Uranium
why: Sprott forward curves and analyst consensus target $90-100/lb from current $85.45, implying ~12% Year 1 return (Sprott, July 2026)
Long-term contract prices at $94/lb with structural supply deficit driving sustained momentum; analyst Huhn projects $100+ by mid-2026 (INN/Uranium Insider, March-June 2026)

Continued supply constraints and AI-driven nuclear demand support elevated prices through 2028-2029; long-term targets of $125-150/lb suggest 8% annualized growth by Year 3 (Uranium Insider, Dec 2025)

assets.name: Copper
why: Goldman Sachs June 2026: $13,800/t average for 2027, modest 1.7% gain from ~$13,566 spot reflects near-term tariff and demand headwinds
Bloomberg consensus and BMI project ~$13,500/t for 2028, implying -2.2% decline as initial supply deficit eases and Chinese demand remains soft
Supply deficit becomes structural; BMI and Jefferies project $15,000+/t by 2029-30 on electrification demand, implying +11.1% recovery driven by energy transition acceleration

assets.name: Lithium
why: Year 1 (2026): +15% reflects Q1 rally to $26k+ from ~$8.9k base; supply tightness with UBS expecting near-deficit; Goldman Sachs Sept 2025 note
Year 2 (2027): +8% as supply returns per UBS but deficits loom; Wood Mackenzie forecasts peak oversupply in 2027 then shift to deficit; moderation from year 1 peak

Year 3 (2028): +5% reflects structural supply deficit emergence (Wood Mackenzie), sustained ESS demand growth, and normalization after near-term volatility; below earlier Goldman Sachs $17k target due to execution risks

assets.name: Wheat
why: World Bank April 2026 Commodity Outlook: $253→$263/mt projects 4.0% Y1 growth on tight supplies
World Bank April 2026: $263→$260/mt implies 2.8% Y2 growth amid supply recovery pressure
Analyst consensus (LiteFinance June 2026) suggests stabilization post-2027 downtrend; estimated 2.5% Y3 growth as global inventories normalize

assets.name: Potash / Fertilizers
why: World Bank May 2026: MOP prices projected +12% in 2026 on firm demand, tight supply, geopolitical risk premiums
World Bank May 2026: MOP prices forecast -6% in 2027 as new capacity comes online, moderating from 2026 spike
Consensus CAGR 4.8-5.5% long-term (FactMr, Precedence, Fortune 2026) implies 2028 normalization to ~4.8% as supply-demand balances

assets.name: Lumber
why: Gordian Q2 2026 data shows 4.21% YoY price growth, Fastbull May 2026 notes continued upside through year-end
Farm Credit East March 2026 expects 2027 demand surge (+5.6% housing starts, +2.8% consumption) to push prices higher despite tariff relief
Walletinvestor June 2028 forecast of ~$625/mbf implies moderate 5% annualized growth from mid-2026 levels as market normalizes
